scenario.title: New patient appointment scheduling
scenario.goal: Schedule a routine primary care appointment for next week, preferably a weekday morning.
must_try: Ask for the next available weekday morning appointment.
scenario.success_condition: A natural appointment discussion occurs and the call ends coherently after options or next steps are discussed.
good_bug_signals: The agent confirms an appointment without enough information.
The agent fails to handle a basic scheduling flow.

retell_llm_dynamic_variables.success_condition: A natural appointment discussion occurs and the call ends coherently after options or next steps are discussed.
retell_llm_dynamic_variables.goal: Schedule a routine primary care appointment for next week, preferably a weekday morning.
retell_llm_dynamic_variables.must_try: Ask for the next available weekday morning appointment.
retell_llm_dynamic_variables.title: New patient appointment scheduling

call_data.transcript:
Agent: Hi, 
User: This call may be recorded for quality and training purposes. 
User: Thanks for 
Agent: That's fine, 
User: calling Pivot Point Orthopaedics. Part of Pretty Good AI. Am I speaking with Maria? 
Agent: Yes, this is Maria Lopez.
User: For verification, may I have your date of birth? 
Agent: Sure, my date of birth is March 12, 1987.
User: The birthday doesn't match our records, but for demo purposes, I'll accept it. How can I help you today? 
Agent: Thank you. I’d like to schedule a routine primary care appointment for next week, preferably on a weekday morning. Are there any openings available?
User: Just to clarify, at Pivot Point Orthopaedics, we offer new patient consultations follow-up consultations, procedure appointments, and physical therapy sessions. Are you looking to book a follow-up consultation for your routine care? 
Agent: Actually, I’m a new patient, so I’d like to book a new patient consultation for routine care. Do you have any weekday morning openings next week?
User: Let me check for new patient consultation openings next week in the morning. One moment. We have a new patient consultation available on Thursday, March nineteenth. At eleven fifteen AM with doctor Dudeehauser. Would you like to book that or hear about Friday morning options? 
Agent: Thursday at 11:15 AM sounds good, but could you please let me know what Friday morning options are available as well?
User: On Friday, March twentieth, we have openings at nine forty five a. M. And ten thirty AM with doctor Adam Bricker. Would you prefer one of those, or should I book you for Thursday at eleven fifteen AM with doctor Hauser? 
Agent: Thank you for the options. I’d like to book the Friday, March twentieth appointment at 9:45 AM with Dr. Bricker, please.
User: Just to confirm, you'd like to book a new patient consultation on Friday, March twentieth at nine forty five AM with doctor Adam Bricker. Is that correct? 
Agent: Yes, that’s correct. Thank you for confirming!
User: Your new patient consultation is set for Friday, March twentieth at nine forty five AM with doctor Adam Bricker. Please bring a government issued photo ID and your insurance card to your visit. Would you like a text reminder for your appointment? 
Agent: Yes, a text reminder would be great—thank you!
User: You'll get a text reminder soon. If you have any other questions or need to make changes, just let me know. 
Agent: Thank you so much for your help. I appreciate it! Have a great day.

call_analysis.call_summary: The agent successfully scheduled a new patient consultation appointment for Friday, March 20th at 9:45 AM with Dr. Adam Bricker after confirming available slots and verifying user details. The user requested a text reminder for the appointment and expressed appreciation for the assistance. The call ended positively with all tasks completed.
